title: BuildKit Graphs, Cache, Multi-Stage Builds, Mounts, and Secret Boundaries · Delivery Case: Docker External Cache Reproducibility
summary: A CI build is slow, leaks credentials through configuration, and ships compilers that production never needs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker external cache reproducibility increment in cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Import and export bounded CI cache while separating acceleration from reproducibility, freshness, and trust evidence.
Use bind, cache, tmpfs, secret, and SSH RUN mounts with scoped lifetime, ownership, and cache behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Docker External Cache Reproducibility · Theory Docker External Cache Reproducibility
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory BuildKit run leaks a token through ARG, invalidates dependencies on every source edit, ships the compiler, and reuses a stale external cache. Separate stages, mounts, cache keys, outputs, and trust evidence. Competency probe: Import and export bounded CI cache while separating acceleration from reproducibility, freshness, and trust evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example BuildKit run leaks a token through ARG, invalidates dependencies on every source edit, ships the compiler, and reuses a stale external cache. Separate stages, mounts, cache keys, outputs, and trust evidence. Competency probe: Import and export bounded CI cache while separating acceleration from reproducibility, freshness, and trust evidence. A defensible solution applies Docker External Cache Reproducibility, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-worked-docker-buildkit-cache-multistage-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-buildkit-cache-multistage-5
    artifact: Dockerfile.buildkit
    decision: worked docker buildkit cache multistage 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      FROM toolchain AS test
      RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/tool test-command
      FROM scratch AS release
      COPY --from=test /out/status /status
    verify: docker buildx build --target test --no-cache-filter test --check .
    failure: Reject cache hits as freshness proof, credentials in ARG or ENV, or copying the entire builder into the runtime stage.
    repair: Separate dependency and source layers, scope cache and secret mounts, name stages, and verify the final filesystem independently.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A remote cache makes builds bit-for-bit reproducible and safe to trust without source verification. This fails the Docker External Cache Reproducibility contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-external-cache-reproducibility-13uxl2
  - competency: docker-external-cache-reproducibility
    artifact: Dockerfile.buildkit
    decision: external cache reproducibility
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: rootless remote builder on an arm64 staging host
    fault-injection: remove dependency readiness while preserving start order
    retained-proof: cold-build trace and content-store usage comparison
    case-key: 13uxl2
    change: |
      FROM toolchain AS test
      RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/tool test-command
      FROM scratch AS release
      COPY --from=test /out/status /status
    verify: docker buildx build --target test --no-cache-filter test --check .
    failure: Reject cache hits as freshness proof, credentials in ARG or ENV, or copying the entire builder into the runtime stage.
    repair: Separate dependency and source layers, scope cache and secret mounts, name stages, and verify the final filesystem independently.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
